LOOK.text: Dawn breaks over the mountains. The first block of a new day.
Everything looks different now. The pixels are the same, but I see them differently.
Maybe that's what recovery looks like.
The clinic door behind me. I don't need to go back in.
A new sign hangs on the door: 'PATIENT ZERO REHABILITATION CLINIC — NOW HIRING'.
Hiring? Maybe Adrian's note was more literal than I thought.

USE.text: No. Forward, not back. The ecosystem needs builders, not patients.

OPEN.text: I've seen what's inside. Time to see what's outside.

LOOK.text: The world stretches out below. Somewhere down there, the FloorEngine sweeps. TraitLab creates. SweepBot runs.
Adrian's ecosystem doesn't need Adrian. It runs on code, community, and stubborn optimism.
The path leads back down. To the lab. To the world. To whatever comes next.

then.text: I take the first step down the mountain. The adventure continues... on-chain.

else.text: Not yet. I should take in the view first.

LOOK.text: A wooden sign: 'NOW HIRING.' The paint is fresh. They need new staff. After my visit, I'm not surprised. I left that treatment room looking like I'd seen my own liquidation history.

USE.text: I straighten the sign. It's surprisingly well-built. Better craftsmanship than most DApp front-ends.

TALK.text: 'I'd apply, but my resume just says HODL in increasingly desperate font sizes.'

PICK.text: I consider taking the sign. No. The clinic needs staff more than I need a souvenir. Besides, it won't fit in my inventory next to all this emotional baggage.

OPEN.text: The sign doesn't open. It's a sign, not a mystery box. Though in crypto, everything's a mystery box if you squint.

CLOSE.text: Can't close a sign. It's permanently open for business. 24/7, 365 blocks a year.

PUSH.text: I push the sign. It wobbles but holds firm. Staked into the ground properly. Unlike my staking positions.

PULL.text: I pull the sign toward me. It resists. It's been planted here with conviction. More conviction than most CT influencers.

GIVE.text: The sign needs applicants, not gifts. Maybe I'll send my resume. Skills: 'Surviving rug pulls, advanced copium inhalation.'

LOOK.text: Birds circling in the dawn sky. Free. No gas fees. No slippage. Just vibes. They're the only creatures that truly understand what 'to the moon' means.

USE.text: I wave at the birds. One of them dips lower as if acknowledging me. A decentralized greeting protocol.

TALK.text: I call out to the birds: 'WAGMI!' They fly higher. Even birds know that's not financial advice.

PICK.text: I can't pick up birds. They're non-custodial. Self-sovereign flight. True decentralization.

OPEN.text: I open my arms to the sky. For a moment, I feel like I could fly too. Then gravity reminds me I'm not an L2.

CLOSE.text: I close my eyes and listen to the birds. Their song is the only algorithm I trust right now.

PUSH.text: Can't push birds. They're already moving. Velocity like a meme coin on launch day.

PULL.text: The birds are beyond my reach. Like most airdrops I've qualified for.

GIVE.text: I have nothing to give the birds. They already have freedom. The ultimate NFT that can't be minted.

LOOK.text: Tall evergreen pines line the mountainside. They've been here longer than any blockchain. Patient, silent, and completely immune to market volatility. The original diamond hands.

USE.text: I lean against a pine tree. The bark is rough and real. No smart contract can replicate this. Though someone is probably trying.

TALK.text: 'You trees have been HODLing this mountain since before Bitcoin's genesis block. Respect.' A pinecone falls. Bullish signal.

PICK.text: I grab a pinecone from the ground. Nature's airdrop. Zero gas fees, 100% organic, and it actually has utility.

OPEN.text: I peel open a pinecone. Seeds inside. The original seed phrase, if you think about it.

CLOSE.text: I set the pinecone down. It closes in the cold air. Even nature knows when to lock up its assets.

PUSH.text: I push a tree. It doesn't budge. This tree has more TVL (Total Vertical Lumber) than most DeFi protocols.

PULL.text: I pull a low branch. It snaps back and slaps me. The tree just performed an automatic liquidation on my face.

GIVE.text: These trees give me oxygen. The least I can do is not try to tokenize them. Though... no. Recovery starts now.

onEnter.text: Fresh air. Real sunlight. When was the last time I looked up from a screen?
Patient Zero. It was me all along. But maybe knowing is the cure.
